creator: deeptech71@gmail.com
text:
There is a test case in which bsdgrep segfaults.

The segfaulty code is in tre_match_fast(), in the "SHIFT;" statement, which contains (via CPP expansion):

  bc = fg->qsBc[((const unsigned char *)str_byte)
                [!fg->reversed ? (size_t)j + fg->len
                               : (size_t)j - 1]];

When the segfault occurs, str_byte points to "" (the empty string), fg->reversed is true, and j is 0. Thus, "" is indexed with UINT_MAX (on this machine).

The triggering input is
- a regular expression of "stderr.txt" as the pattern, and
- a somewhat reduced version of my "libgcj.a" file (uploaded as "hax.dat", actually, compressed as "hax.dat.xz") as the data.

Fix: Patch attached with submission follows:
How-To-Repeat: $ xz --decompress hax.dat.xz
$ bsdgrep stderr.txt hax.dat
Segmentation fault (core dumped)
